stepAnswer: The decision boundary is the plane, curve, or line that separates the classification of one class from another.
stepTitle: b) See Image. Draw the decision boundary that the k-nearest neighbors algorithm (with k $$=$$ 5) would generate for this problem.
choices: The decision boundary is the line that minimizes the total Euclidean distance between all benign points and all malignant points, splitting the data into two $$equal-sized$$ groups.
The decision boundary is drawn by connecting the $$5$$ nearest neighbors of every point in the dataset and tracing the midpoints between them.
The decision boundary is the plane, curve, or line that separates the classification of one class from another.
